failure_surface: Logical mismatch in cross-sheet matching direction; row insertion requirement not implemented
root_cause: The agent reversed the matching logic: instead of checking if Sheet2 column headers exist in Sheet1's Entry column values, it checked if hardcoded prefixes in Sheet1 Entry values existed as Sheet2 column names. Additionally, the required operation to insert rows proportional to Sheet2's height was completely omitted.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When implementing cross-sheet matching, verify the comparison direction matches the instruction specification exactly, and implement ALL explicit structural operations (such as row insertion) rather than substituting them with alternative operations like appending.
evidence: Instruction states: 'check if a header from Sheet2 exists in the Entry column of Sheet1' but code checks hardcoded prefixes against Sheet2.columns
Instruction requires: 'insert the same number of rows as there are in Sheet2 for that header' but no openpyxl.insert_rows() or similar was called
Agent substituted row insertion with direct data writing to existing cells via ws3[f'A{i+1}'] = val
Hardcoded form prefixes in code ('U4_ComponentPropertyForm', etc.) were not derived from Sheet2 headers dynamically
Multiple syntax error attempts suggest confusion about code block formatting before correct execution

memory_items.title: Match Direction Verification
memory_items.lesson: When performing cross-sheet lookups, explicitly verify which sheet provides the lookup keys and which sheet contains the search space
memory_items.generalization: Before implementing matching logic, write down: 'X from [source_sheet] matches Y in [target_sheet]' to confirm directionality aligns with instructions

memory_items.title: Complete Structural Operations Checklist
memory_items.lesson: Explicitly enumerate all structural modifications requested (insert rows, delete columns, merge cells) and verify each is implemented separately from data population
memory_items.generalization: Create a checklist mapping instruction verbs to concrete operations before coding, ensuring append/insert/copy/delete actions are all covered

memory_items.title: Dynamic Header Extraction Over Hardcoded Values
memory_items.lesson: When instructions reference headers from another sheet, extract them programmatically rather than assuming known values
memory_items.generalization: Always use df.columns.tolist() or wb[sheet].columns to retrieve available headers dynamically instead of maintaining hardcoded lists that may drift from actual file contents

edits.content:
## Cross-Sheet Matching Patterns

### Directional Verification
- When matching data across worksheets, explicitly document the source and target of each lookup operation
- Phrase requirements as '[value_source] from Sheet A matches [search_space] in Sheet B'
- Verify against instruction wording before coding any join or filter logic

### Structural Operation Tracking
- Create a checklist of ALL explicit worksheet modifications (row insertion, column deletion, cell merging)
- Ensure structural operations execute BEFORE data population to maintain proper row indexing
- Never substitute row insertion with data appending unless explicitly permitted

### Dynamic Data Dependencies
- Extract all referenced headers/columns programmatically using df.columns or worksheet.cell references
- Avoid hardcoded identifiers when the instruction references external worksheet content
- Validate extracted schema against instruction expectations before proceeding
